title: Ipomoea leucanthemum
taxonomy: Ipomoea leucanthemum is classified within the plant family Convolvulaceae. It belongs to the genus Ipomoea, which comprises approximately 600 species of flowering plants. The species Ipomoea leucanthemum is native to tropical and subtropical regions, and its classification is further divided into the family level, genus level, and species level. The species is part of the Ipomoea genus, which is characterized by its bindweed-like growth habit and trumpet-shaped flowers. The classification of Ipomoea leucanthemum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Convolvulaceae family.

intro: Ipomoea leucanthemum, also known as Convolvulus leucanthemus, is a flowering plant species within the Convolvulaceae family. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea leucanthemum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Ipomoea leucanthemum is prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and is often combined with other herbs to create remedies for various health complaints.
